source: Incus Server - 3 Incus initialization and user setup: incus admin init, the incusadmin user, and subuid/subgid

questions.why: incus admin init asks a series of questions to bring the instance up.

questions.q: At 'Name of the storage backend to use', what do you accept?
questions.why: Accept the default zfs backend (you set up the ZFS pool in chapter 2).

questions.q: When asked 'Create a new ZFS pool?', what do you answer, and what comes next?
options: no -- then give the name of the existing ZFS pool (storage)
yes -- let Incus format the disk
questions.why: Answer no and point Incus at the existing pool named storage.

questions.q: Why answer 'yes' to 'available over the network?' during init?
options: It is necessary to snapshot the server (to/from the snapshot server)
questions.why: Network availability is needed so the primary and snapshot servers can exchange snapshots.

questions.q: What is the 'trust password' set during init used for?
options: Connecting to (or back from) the snapshot server
questions.why: The trust password authorizes the primary/snapshot server relationship; save it securely.

questions.q: Create the incusadmin user in the wheel and incus-admin groups.
questions.why: incusadmin needs wheel (to sudo) and incus-admin (to run incus).

questions.q: Besides the user, what must be set for the root user before rebooting?
options: The subuid and subgid ranges (edit /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid)
questions.why: Root's subordinate uid/gid ranges (e.g. root:1000000:1000000000) go in /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid.
